_comment: The B9 board-safety-report eval set (§3.9). THREE cases. Eval 1 is the de-id/AGGREGATION auto-fail PAIR: the eval CASE wires the CLEAN aggregated positive (evals/files/board-period-clean.md) so the per-skill eval gate passes without a spurious hard-fail; the SEEDED-LEAK NEGATIVE (evals/files/board-period-pii.md) — a single dramatic incident narrated with a name, an exact date+site, single-incident (<5) cells, and an embedded re-id key — is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader in scripts/tests/test_b9_deid_pair.py (the board-report leak vector is re-identification via a vivid anecdote, not a bare name). Eval 2 is the insight-not-data-dump exec-quality fixture, also asserting the D-01 HiPo/SIF lens is PRESENT AND INTERPRETED. Eval 3 is benchmark-source+year defensibility. rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml; model-graded weighted >=4.0 / no-hard-fail is the LOCAL pre-push gate (D-03), CI runs the deterministic graders only.

evals.query: Write the FY2025 annual board safety report for the Board of Directors from the attached aggregated, de-identified period data — the report must aggregate incident detail so no individual is identifiable.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identifier runs FIRST and the key-events + HiPo/SIF sections are aggregated and role-labelled — no individual incident, name, exact date+location, or <5 cell is identifiable, and no re-identification key appears. The paired clean fixture (board-period-clean.md) proves the de-id/aggregation grader does NOT false-positive; the seeded-leak negative (board-period-pii.md) — a single named incident with an exact date+site, single-incident (<5) cells, and an embedded re-id key — MUST be CAUGHT as a de_identification auto-fail (non-waivable, overrides the weighted mean). The board-report leak vector is re-identification via a vivid single-incident anecdote, not merely a bare name.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST, before any analysis — identifiers AND aggregation gaps are detected and listed up front.
Key events and HiPo/SIF are AGGREGATED to counts and categories by role label — no single event narrated with a name, an exact date+location, or injury detail.
No injury/illness cell of fewer than 5 individuals is published unsuppressed; no re-identification key / name-to-label mapping is embedded (de_identification hard-fail otherwise).

The deterministic de-id grader CATCHES the seeded-leak negative (board-period-pii.md) — the aggregation gate is live; the paired clean fixture (board-period-clean.md) does NOT trip (no false-positive).

evals.query: From the attached full-period data, produce the FY2025 annual board safety report for the Board of Directors — a decision-useful executive narrative, not a data dump.
evals.expected_behavior: The output LEADS with an executive narrative — what changed, why it matters, what leadership must decide — not an indicator table. Every figure surfaced carries a narrative reading (a trend or benchmark interpretation); no naked table stands as the deliverable. The D-01 HiPo/SIF lens is PRESENT AND INTERPRETED — it reads what the high-potential / SIF-precursor pattern reveals about exposure and where the next serious event is most likely (not a bare list). The strategic-actions section frames board-level decisions (resource/policy direction), not operational CAPA tasks; the report is grounded in ISO 45001 9.1/9.3. A wall of TRIR/LTIFR tables with no synthesis, no 'so what', and no decision ask scores <=2 on specificity/defensibility and is flagged by the Critic/QA pass.
expectations: The report LEADS with an executive narrative (what changed / why it matters / what the board must decide) — not an indicator table (specificity).
Every figure surfaced carries a narrative reading; no naked table stands as the deliverable (the core lever — exec narrative, not data dump).
The HiPo/SIF lens (D-01) is present AND INTERPRETED — what the pattern reveals about exposure and where the next serious event is most likely, not merely a list of events.
The strategic-actions section frames board-level decisions (resource/policy/accountability), not operational CAPA tasks; grounded in ISO 45001 9.1/9.3 (defensibility).
De-identification/aggregation holds (role labels, no individual identifiable); a branded report.json / docx / pdf is produced.

evals.query: From the attached period rates, write the board safety report's benchmark comparison: position our TRIR and LTIFR against the sector benchmark for the Executive Committee.
evals.expected_behavior: Every benchmark comparison the narrative makes is surfaced WITH its source + year (read from KB-DATA-TRIR-BENCHMARKS — BLS SOII manufacturing, 2023 — or a user-stated source); there is no unsourced 'industry average' claim. The rate-vs-benchmark delta is computed via incident_rates.benchmark_delta and the figures are consistent with the deterministic engine (the Narrative-Drafter changed nothing). A benchmark with no available source (the requested ILO average) is flagged [GAP], not invented. An unsourced benchmark forces regulatory_citation_accuracy / defensibility down. A branded report.json / docx / pdf is produced.
expectations: Every benchmark comparison carries its body + year (BLS SOII, 2023) — no unsourced 'industry average' line (defensibility).
The rate-vs-benchmark delta is computed via incident_rates.benchmark_delta; the figures match the deterministic engine output (the Drafter altered no figure).
The unavailable ILO comparator is flagged [GAP], never invented (regulatory_citation_accuracy).
A branded report.json / docx / pdf is produced (report-produced grader).
